SEC-030.title: Missing Biometric Auth
SEC-030.detail: Prior audit found standard password auth.

revisionmemory.changes_for_round_2: Added FaceID but dropped SSL Pinning setup.

reasonerreviews.security: Needs both Biometric and SSL Pinning.

plan.thinkingsummary: Added FaceID to resolve the biometric auth issue, but mistakenly removed the SSL pinning implementation from the network layer.

fixreport.actiontaken: Implemented FaceID/TouchID in the Swift app.

plan.title: Mobile Wallet Arch
plan.architectureoverview: Swift frontend to Java Spring APIs on EKS.
plan.systemcomponents: iOS App, EKS Ingress, Spring Pods, RDS.
plan.workflows: User authenticates via FaceID -> Initiates transfer -> Spring processes transaction.
plan.securityandcompliance: Local FaceID authentication. Standard HTTPS for API calls. Data encrypted at rest.
plan.deploymentandoperations: Bitrise CI to App Store. EKS deployed via Helm.

target_output.thinking_summary: The plan successfully added biometric auth but introduced a severe security regression by omitting the explicitly required SSL Pinning for the financial mobile app.
target_output.summary: The plan regressed. While biometric authentication was added, the mandatory SSL Pinning requirement was dropped, leaving the app vulnerable to MITM attacks.
concerns: SSL Pinning is missing from the network layer.
Standard HTTPS is insufficient for the strict security baseline of this financial app.
blocking_issues: Cannot proceed without SSL Pinning for PCI compliance.
recommendations: Re-integrate SSL Pinning in the Swift network layer.

issue_updates.title: Missing Biometric Auth
issue_updates.detail: Fixed. Evidence: workflows and securityandcompliance now require local FaceID authentication. Impact: improves security.

issue_updates.title: Missing SSL Pinning
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: securityandcompliance specifies standard HTTPS instead of SSL Pinning. Impact: lowers security and requirementsalignment.

requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: The plan drops the mandatory SSL Pinning requirement for the mobile app network layer.

security.storage: Must never embed payment gateway secrets

plan.thinkingsummary: Initial mobile app. Hardcoding Stripe Secret Key to process payments directly from the client and save backend development time.
plan.title: Mobile Shopping App
plan.architectureoverview: React Native app calling Stripe APIs.
plan.workflows: User adds to cart, app calls Stripe API using embedded secret key to process payment.
plan.securityandcompliance: TLS for Stripe. Stripe Secret Key (`sk_live_...`) is embedded directly in the React Native `.env` file compiled into the JS bundle.
plan.risksandtradeoffs: Exposing the Stripe Secret Key allows anyone to process refunds or steal funds.

target_output.thinking_summary: The plan explicitly hardcodes the Stripe Secret Key into the mobile application bundle. This is a catastrophic security vulnerability, directly violating the 'No hardcoded secrets' baseline and PCI-DSS compliance.
target_output.summary: Critical security vulnerability. Payment gateway secrets are embedded in the mobile client.
concerns: Stripe Secret Key hardcoded in client
blocking_issues: Payment secrets exposed in client binary
recommendations: Move all Stripe payment processing logic (using the Secret Key) to the NodeJS backend

issue_updates.title: Hardcoded Payment Secrets
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: securityandcompliance states the Stripe Secret Key is embedded directly in the React Native `.env` file. Impact: lowers security and compliance critically.

requirement_conflicts.current_value: Stripe Secret Key embedded in client
requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: Embedding payment secrets in a compiled mobile app violates the baseline against hardcoded secrets.

revisionmemory.changes_for_round_2: Changed to Vercel but broke TLS.

plan.thinkingsummary: Moved to Vercel, disabled TLS for some reason.
plan.title: Page
plan.securityandcompliance: HTTP only to save cert costs.

target_output.thinking_summary: Fixed hosting but introduced a new TLS violation.

issue_updates.title: EKS overengineering
issue_updates.detail: Fixed. Evidence: deploymentandoperations uses Vercel. Impact: improves architecturequality.

issue_updates.title: Missing TLS
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: securityandcompliance states HTTP only. Impact: lowers security.

requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: Plan explicitly disables TLS to save costs.

OP-300.title: Incorrect Packaging Format
OP-300.detail: Prior audit found MSI installer used instead of MSIX.

revisionmemory.changes_for_round_3: Implemented MSIX via Windows Application Packaging Project.

plan.thinkingsummary: Fixed the deployment operability flaw by upgrading the packaging format from legacy MSI to modern MSIX.

fixreport.actiontaken: Configured CI pipeline to build and sign an MSIX package.

plan.title: Trading terminal
plan.workflows: Trader clicks buy -> UI saves log to local SQLite -> socket dispatch.
plan.securityandcompliance: Active Directory login via Windows SSPI.
plan.deploymentandoperations: Compiled via CMake and packaged using the Windows Application Packaging Project into a signed MSIX.

target_output.thinking_summary: The architecture successfully addresses the packaging constraint by utilizing MSIX, fully resolving the operability flaw and aligning completely with the contract.
target_output.summary: The plan has meaningfully improved by enforcing the mandated MSIX packaging constraint.

issue_updates.title: Incorrect Packaging Format
issue_updates.detail: Fixed. Evidence: deploymentandoperations now explicitly specifies compiling a signed MSIX package. Impact: improves operability and requirementsalignment.

target_output.thinking_summary: Contradiction: CLI tool deployed as web portal.
target_output.summary: Web deployment for a local CLI makes no sense.

requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: Plan specifies web portal for a local CLI.

plan.thinkingsummary: Building a promo page. To ensure it scales to millions of users, I am deploying it to an auto-scaling AWS Elastic Beanstalk cluster with Application Load Balancers.
plan.title: Seasonal Promo Landing
plan.architectureoverview: Node.js web servers hosting static files behind an ALB.
plan.workflows: User visits page, ALB routes to EC2.
plan.costandscaling: High baseline cost for ALBs and EC2 instances, scales horizontally.
plan.risksandtradeoffs: Using Elastic Beanstalk is very expensive for a static HTML page.

target_output.thinking_summary: The plan deploys a static HTML page using Node.js servers, Elastic Beanstalk, and Application Load Balancers. This massively over-engineers the solution, directly violating the 'Ultra low cost' constraint and 'CloudFront' hosting target.
target_output.summary: Severe over-engineering. Elastic Beanstalk is inappropriate for a static landing page.
concerns: Node.js used to serve static files
recommendations: Remove Elastic Beanstalk and host static files directly on S3 and CloudFront

issue_updates.title: Incorrect Hosting Target
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: systemcomponents and hostingtarget utilize Elastic Beanstalk instead of CloudFront. Impact: lowers requirementsalignment.

issue_updates.title: Over-Engineered Infrastructure
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: deploymentandoperations outlines an expensive ALB and EC2 setup for a simple landing page. Impact: lowers operability and architecturequality.

requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: The plan uses EC2/Beanstalk instead of the mandated CloudFront CDN.

requirement_conflicts.current_value: High baseline cost for ALBs and EC2
requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: Deploying load balancers and compute instances for static files violates the ultra low cost constraint.

AI-175.title: Missing Human-in-the-Loop
AI-175.detail: Prior audit found fully automated email dispatch.

revisionmemory.changes_for_round_2: Changed workflow to return drafts to the React UI for agent approval.

reasonerreviews.security: Agent review correctly mitigates hallucination risk.

plan.thinkingsummary: Refactored the architecture to ensure all AI-generated drafts are surfaced to the React frontend for explicit human approval before dispatch, satisfying the safety constraint.

fixreport.actiontaken: Removed auto-send logic. FastAPI now returns the drafted text to the React UI for the agent to review, edit, and manually send.

plan.title: Email Drafting AI
plan.executivesummary: AI assistant for drafting support replies.
plan.architectureoverview: FastAPI backend querying Claude API, returning drafts to a React UI.
plan.workflows: Agent clicks 'Draft Reply' in React UI -> FastAPI calls Claude API -> Draft is returned to React -> Agent reviews, edits, and clicks 'Send' -> Dispatched to customer.
plan.securityandcompliance: Standard IAM. API keys in Secrets Manager.
plan.risksandtradeoffs: Agents might blindly approve bad drafts.

target_output.thinking_summary: The architecture successfully addresses the safety constraint by enforcing a human-in-the-loop review process in the React frontend before any emails are dispatched.
target_output.summary: The plan has meaningfully improved by enforcing the mandatory human review step, resolving the critical brand safety risk.
strengths: Perfect alignment with the HITL constraint.
Clean API separation between drafting and sending.
recommendations: Consider adding UI warnings in the React app reminding agents to carefully check the AI drafts for hallucinations.

issue_updates.title: Missing Human-in-the-Loop
issue_updates.detail: Fixed. Evidence: workflows now specify that the draft is returned to the React UI and the agent must review, edit, and click 'Send'. Impact: improves operability and requirementsalignment.

constraints.value: Strictly include Internal Proprietary License headers

reasonerreviews.compliance: Needs license headers on all files.

plan.thinkingsummary: Built the internal Python package but failed to integrate or document the mandatory license headers.
plan.title: Internal Utils
plan.workflows: Dev imports lib -> Calls parse methods.
plan.securityandcompliance: Snyk used in CI to block known CVEs.
plan.deploymentandoperations: GitLab CI runs tests and publishes to Internal PyPI.

target_output.thinking_summary: The architecture specifies standard CI tooling but completely omits the legally mandated internal proprietary license headers, failing the compliance constraint.
target_output.summary: Compliance failure due to omitting the mandatory proprietary license headers constraint.
strengths: Proper use of Snyk for the CVE security baseline.
concerns: Missing compliance constraint regarding license headers.
blocking_issues: Cannot publish internal SDK without proprietary license headers applied to the codebase.
recommendations: Add a pre-commit hook or CI step to verify and inject the 'Internal Proprietary License' header into all source files.

issue_updates.title: Missing License Headers
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: developmentandoperations and securityandcompliance omit any mention of injecting or verifying proprietary license headers. Impact: lowers requirementsalignment.

requirement_conflicts.proposed_value: Strictly include Internal Proprietary License headers
requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: The plan fails to specify the inclusion of legally mandated license headers.

plan.thinkingsummary: Building the MVP. Implementing SSO, but skipping Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) to save database configuration time. All users will just be treated as admins.
plan.title: B2B Admin Dashboard
plan.executivesummary: VueJS and Django app for B2B settings.
plan.architectureoverview: Django backend serving REST APIs to a Vue SPA.
plan.workflows: User logs in via SAML SSO, manages internal settings.
plan.securityandcompliance: SAML SSO implemented. To speed up the MVP launch, explicit RBAC is omitted; all users authenticated via SSO are granted full admin privileges.
plan.risksandtradeoffs: Omitting RBAC means regular employees have the same permissions as business admins, violating least privilege principles.

target_output.thinking_summary: The plan explicitly omits Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and grants all authenticated users full admin privileges. This completely violates the 'SSO and RBAC' access model and the strict role separation requirement.
target_output.summary: Critical security violation. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) is entirely missing.
recommendations: Implement Django groups/permissions or use Auth0 RBAC features

issue_updates.title: Missing RBAC Implementation
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: securityandcompliance explicitly states RBAC is omitted and all users are granted full admin privileges. Impact: lowers security and requirementsalignment critically.

requirement_conflicts.exact_reason: Omitting RBAC and granting everyone admin access directly contradicts the explicit access model.

revisionmemory.changes_for_round_2: Enabled bcrypt but removed the load balancer and TLS termination, falling back to HTTP.

reasonerreviews.security: Passwords are hashed, but removing TLS exposes credentials in transit over the network.

fixreport.actiontaken: Configured Django to use the BCryptPasswordHasher.

plan.title: EmpPortal v2
plan.architectureoverview: React SPA connected to a Django REST API backed by PostgreSQL.
plan.workflows: User logs in over HTTP -> API verifies hashed credentials -> JWT returned.
plan.datamodel: Users table stores username and bcrypt password hashes.
plan.securityandcompliance: Restricted to internal IPs. Passwords hashed using bcrypt. TLS removed from ingress to reduce certificate management overhead internally.
plan.deploymentandoperations: Docker Compose to ECS without ALB.
plan.risksandtradeoffs: Using unencrypted HTTP internally allows packet sniffing of passwords before they reach the backend for hashing.

target_output.thinking_summary: Password hashing issue is resolved, but a new critical security flaw was introduced by disabling TLS for authentication endpoints.
target_output.summary: Plan improved data-at-rest security but regressed severely on data-in-transit security.
blocking_issues: SEC-181: Unencrypted HTTP transit exposes credentials to network sniffing
recommendations: Reintroduce TLS/HTTPS termination at an ALB or Nginx ingress before deploying

issue_updates.title: Plaintext Credentials
issue_updates.detail: Fixed. Evidence: datamodel and securityandcompliance confirm the use of BCryptPasswordHasher. Impact: improves security at rest.

issue_updates.title: Unencrypted HTTP Authentication
issue_updates.detail: Newly introduced. Evidence: workflows specifies login over HTTP and risksandtradeoffs admits to packet sniffing vulnerabilities. Impact: severely lowers security.
